title: Reis tablets
wikitext:
[]: Rei's tablets             Quote Post by Jayfive » Tue Nov 30, 2010 9:53 am The tablets we see on Rei's table in Ep5 and elsewhere - Is this something thats been covered in depth before? Id be interested to hear what people things they are or represent? Me? Two theories. 1) Painkillers for her injuries suffered a few weeks before. Basically a daily dose of Occam's razor. Could be representative of the lengths NERV will go to to get the kids in the EVAs if needed. 2) Going on the theory that sync rates and so on are somehow linked to early teenage years and the fact that Asuka's inability to pilot Unit 02 coincides with her getting her period, that these are some sort of medicines to stave off puberty or specifically menstruation. And all the symbolic jiggery-pokery of that should be self evident :P Are there any other theories out there?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Nov 30, 2010 9:59 am She's a clone with health problems?

[]: Re: Rei's tablets             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Nov 30, 2010 10:17 am Jayfive wrote: 2) Going on the theory that sync rates and so on are somehow linked to early teenage years and the fact that Asuka's inability to pilot Unit 02 coincides with her getting her period, that these are some sort of medicines to stave off puberty or specifically menstruation. And all the symbolic jiggery-pokery of that should be self evident :P

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Tue Nov 30, 2010 10:37 am That theory's explicitly debunked in the show.

[]:             Quote Post by tehprognoob » Tue Nov 30, 2010 10:46 am random vitamins and supplements? drugs? LSD? (just kidding)

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Nov 30, 2010 11:04 am Jayfive wrote: It is? Point me in that direction :)

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Nov 30, 2010 11:07 am Hyper Shinchan wrote: Ritsuko says that the synchronisation isn't affected by the period.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Nov 30, 2010 11:24 am ^ Yeah, you're right; I wasn't more specific only because it wasn't the point of the discussion; to be even more specific she says "superficial physiological conditions" (at least in the ADV's Platinum edition).

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Tue Nov 30, 2010 11:44 am Hyper Shinchan wrote: Actually yesterday I was noticing that Rei takes pain killers even when she's not injured (in 2.22 before the scene where Shinji and Asuka are fighting because he didn't make a bento), it's quite possible that she may have a need for that stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Nov 30, 2010 11:54 am thewayneiac wrote: You really shouldn't try to apply anything in rebuild to the original series canon.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Nov 30, 2010 12:22 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: More specifically physiological changes. So periods, wounds, sickness, etc wouldn't affect anything. Now psychological changes, that does affect the synch.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Nov 30, 2010 12:28 pm Bagheera wrote: Although, that said, I've always found this to be really odd. Obviously wounds, sickness, and other physiological conditions affect one's mental stability and ability to focus and such. Since the ability to synchronize with an Eva seems to involve focusing on and generally harmonizing with the unit it would seem that anything that affects your mental state could mess up the process -- up to and including wounds, sickness, periods, etc.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Nov 30, 2010 12:38 pm It wasn't like she just had her menarche, probably until that point she had many and many tests during her period but they didn't affect her results (or at least they didn't affect it in a relevant way).

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Nov 30, 2010 12:39 pm Azathoth wrote: Synchronization is a soul thing. It's entirely possible that souls in NGE aren't negatively affected by the condition of the bodies in which they reside - they seem amenable to losing said body entirely, after all. But yeah, the mind/body dualism in Eva seems a little silly.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Nov 30, 2010 1:03 pm Bagheera wrote: We don't know that (though it seems likely

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Nov 30, 2010 1:17 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: In the manga

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Nov 30, 2010 1:34 pm Bagheera wrote: That's very nice for the manga. Means fuck all for the anime.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Nov 30, 2010 1:50 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: I'm bored of this attitude of EGF's members, if it's not explicitly against NGE and it can be supported by hints in NGE why do you want to throw it out completely just like it doesn't even exist?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Nov 30, 2010 1:50 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: I'm bored of this attitude of EGF's members, if it's not explicitly against NGE and it can be supported by hints in NGE why do you want to throw out it completely just like it doesn't even exist?

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Nov 30, 2010 2:13 pm Bagheera wrote: Because the manga is demonstrably different from the series in many respects, and thus shouldn't be used as evidence for things that happen within the series? It's not just expanded material, after all. It's completely different in many important plot points. I see no more reason to give them any credence than any other non-series work (fanfic, games, etc).
